query: Our credit-risk model has been in production for 6 months. AUC has dropped from 0.83 to 0.74 over the last quarter. We don't have drift monitoring set up yet. Help me build a per-feature drift monitor and find what shifted. We have the original training set as a reference baseline and 14 days of recent inference logs as the current window. Features include applicant_income (continuous), employment_tenure_months (continuous), loan_purpose (categorical), and credit_score_band (ordinal).
expected_behavior: Recommends per-feature drift metrics matched to feature type — PSI (or KS) for continuous features and Jensen-Shannon (or chi-squared) for the categorical loan_purpose, instead of one global metric across all features

Names baseline-noise calibration (computing the drift metric across multiple reference subwindows) as a required step before setting alert thresholds, rather than hard-coding a fixed PSI band like 0.1 or 0.25

Frames the AUC drop investigation as requiring root-cause attribution across at least three hypothesis classes (instrumentation bug, cohort-mix shift, real population drift) before recommending retraining
